name: Lowry Theatre
description: Welcome aboard. You are joining us at Salford Quays, formerly known as the Manchester Docks. The building to the right is the Lowry Theatre and art gallery, opened in 2000 by Queen Elizabeth II.

name: Coronation Street & ITV
description: ITV and Coronation Street moved here in 2013. Coronation Street first aired on 9th December 1960 and holds the title of the world's longest-running TV soap.

name: Mode Wheel Locks
description: The last of five locks on the 35-mile journey from Eastham. Queen Victoria officially opened the Manchester Ship Canal here in 1894. Ships drop 18 metres to sea level.

name: MediaCity / BBC
description: Home to the BBC, who relocated here in 2011. The studio complex is known as Dock 10 — from 100-year-old plans to build a new dock here, abandoned with the First World War.

name: Imperial War Museum North
description: Designed by Daniel Libeskind in the deconstructivist style — imagined as three interlocking shards of a globe shattered by conflict. Opened July 2002.

name: Old Trafford
description: Home to Manchester United since 1910. Record attendance of 76,962 in 1939 for an FA Cup semi-final between Wolverhampton Wanderers and Grimsby Town.

name: Soapworks
description: Once the Ivy Soap Works, established 1874. Acquired by Colgate-Palmolive in 1938. The preserved red brick chimney is a reminder of Salford's soap-making heritage.

name: Mark Addy Bridge
description: Named after local hero Mark Addy, who saved over 50 people from the polluted River Irwell in the 19th century. Also marks the boundary between the Ship Canal and the River Irwell.

name: Trinity Islands
description: One of Manchester's most recent residential developments, with towers rising up to 69 storeys. Part of the reason the city skyline is nicknamed MancHatten.

name: Stephenson's Bridge
description: A Grade II listed stone bridge from 1830. Part of the world's first passenger railway line connecting Manchester to Liverpool, departing from nearby Liverpool Road Station.

name: Graffiti Palace
description: A stretch of riverside wall that has become an ever-changing canvas of street art. Artists from across Manchester come here to leave their mark — always evolving.

name: Quay West
description: A glass-fronted office block built in the early 1990s, once home to Peel Holdings who own the Manchester Ship Canal. Marks the end of your journey through Manchester's waterfront.
